fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 50-year-old African American female with schizophrenia with moderate persistent symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Lewis attended appointment with son who assists with treatment. Son confirms she takes quetiapine 600 mg nightly via his supervision, no missed doses. Despite high-dose medication and good adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptoms. Reports daily auditory hallucinations of voice commenting negatively on her actions. Endorses paranoid beliefs that neighbors monitor her apartment and report on her to authorities. Sleep fair at 5-6 hours nightly. Appetite decreased, weight stable at 162 lbs. Not working, on disability for past eight years. Lives alone with son visiting three times weekly. Attends community mental health day program twice weekly. Limited social contacts. Mood 'nervous' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Son reports her symptoms are stable but persistent. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by LaShawn Williams, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Social Worker)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged female, simple clothing, adequate grooming, appears guarded
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Daily auditory hallucinations, paranoid delusions about surveillance

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC normal; glucose 122 mg/dL; lipids elevated.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives alone with family support; unable to work, on disability; limited social engagement; regular day program attendance.
Assessment_and_Plan: Moderate symptoms persistent on maximum quetiapine dose.
Will transition to different antipsychotic - starting paliperidone ER.
fields.Author: Note written by LaShawn Williams, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Social Worker — Community Mental Health Day Program, 5521 Wellness Center Drive, Philadelphia, PA 19102, Phone: (555) 992-6643, Fax: (555) 992-6644
